$comment: D-CSUBSET-INT-FLOAT-CONVERSION: the int<->float implicit assignment conversion, BOTH directions, in EVERY context. Frontier: sqlite kahanBabuskaNeumaierStep(pSum, iBig) passes an i64 to a `volatile double` param -> S0003 S_TypeMismatch (DSS had NO int<->float arm in isAssignable; even `double d=5;` failed). GAP 1 adds intConvertsToFloat / floatConvertsToInt gate bools + the two isAssignable arms, threaded through all 5 semantic call sites (init x2 / assign / call-arg / return). GAP 2 wires the codegen: x86 cvtsi2sd (F2 [REX.W] 0F 2A) + arm64 SCVTF (0x9E620000 Dd,Xn / 0x1E620000 Dd,Wn) for int->double; the existing cvttsd2si/FCVTZS serves double->int. The int->float DEST is fixed F64 this cycle (source-int width owns the single guard axis -> REX.W/Wn-vs-Xn exact); int->F32 fails loud (D-CSUBSET-INT-TO-F32-CODEGEN deferred). Inputs via io() stay runtime so the optimized arm runs the real cvtsi2sd/SCVTF. iBig=1000000003 (>2^30, <2^53 so exact in f64) proves the i64-source width; the double->int `int t=dv` truncates 5.5->5 (NOT 6) proving cvttsd2si truncation. a+b+c+d = 30+7+0+5 = 42. RED-ON-DISABLE: flip either gate to false in c-subset.lang.json -> that direction's implicit assignment fails S0003 -> does not compile. arm64 RUN is verified on qemu (the SCVTF fixedWords were computed, cross-checked vs the config's FCVTZS field decode, and need on-hardware confirmation — flagged for the audit).
